Vega™, from Borealis, is a packaged facility cooling platform compressed into a white-space footprint. It deploys like a CRAC unit and delivers cold water at the load — one governed package carrying the full thermal path from rack-side delivery through site-flexible heat rejection.

The Discipline
Thirty-five years of cooling the heat load at the heat load directly. Vega is the facility-class expression of that discipline.
Vega™
AGT-USA Inc.
The Architecture Insight

We moved the chiller plant inside, next to the compute.

The industry knows how to deploy a CRAC unit. Vega adopts that deployment model — and delivers the output of a facility chiller.

A CRAC unit sits inside the hall, connects to building services, and conditions the space. Packaged. Predictable. Serviced by the mechanical contractors already on site. Vega takes that deployment model and scales the output by an order of magnitude.

Each Vega unit is a complete facility cooling plant in about 27 square feet per 500 kW — vapor compression, variable-speed pumping, filtration, controls, and rack-side heat delivery, packaged inside the white space. Heat rejection sits outside. Cold water gets produced at the load.

The market typically buys chiller, CDU, pumping, heat rejection, and controls as separate systems. Vega delivers them as one.

Front-access service. Separate refrigerant circuits. The facility-plant reliability model, packaged.
Deployment · Output
Deploys Like
A CRAC unit — packaged, white-space deployable, serviced by on-site mechanical contractors.
Delivers Like
A facility chiller plant — because it is one. Vapor compression, pumping, filtration, heat rejection. Unified supervisory control across the entire package.
Scope
Full thermal path. Rack-side delivery through site-flexible heat rejection, delivered as one package.
Internal ViewVapor compression circuit, hydraulic distribution, and integrated controls. The facility chiller, compressed to the white space.
Why It Matters
Once cold water exists at the load, rear-door, direct-to-chip, and mixed white-space cooling become deployable.
Rear-door heat exchangers. Direct-to-chip cold plates. Mixed deployments. The white-space infrastructure that cold water enables. Continued on the next page →

What Cold Water Enables

Two delivery paths. One thermal path.

Cold water at the load needs a place to land. Vega supports two rack-side delivery paths — the AGT Rear-Door Heat Exchanger as the designed matched pair, or a Customer-Preferred Rear-Door Heat Exchanger as a supported alternative. Either way, unified supervisory control governs the integrated system. One thermal path, from the rack surface to the heat rejection, whichever door is on the rack.

AGT Rear-Door Heat Exchanger Active or Passive · Up to 70 kW per door · Matched Pair Option
The Thermal
Interface
Vega delivers facility water at specified supply temperature and flow. Rack-side heat extraction happens in the architecture the site chooses.
Rear-door heat exchangers  ·  Direct-to-chip, single- or two-phase  ·  Enclosed thermal cabinets  ·  Immersion
Rack-Side Delivery Configurations — RDHx
Designed Matched Pair
AGT Rear-Door Heat Exchanger
  • Active or passive, up to 70 kW per door
  • AGT-designed, AGT-integrated, warranted as a matched pair
  • One manufacturer across the thermal path
  • Single service channel, single warranty
  • Fastest path from order to deployment
Unified Control
One authority across either path
Supported Alternative
Customer-Preferred Rear-Door Heat Exchanger
  • Vega supports customer-selected RDHx platforms at the thermal interface
  • Customer retains preferred vendor relationship
  • Supports facility standardization requirements
  • Unified control across the integrated system
  • Same thermal path performance, existing standards
Cold Water Also Enables
01
Mixed Deployments
RDHx and DTC in the same governed loop. Migrate liquid-ready racks incrementally.
02
Direct-to-Chip
Cold plates connect to Vega's supply loop for chip-level cooling at extreme densities.
03
White-Space Infrastructure
Enclosures, PDUs, UPS, and emergency systems deploy alongside the thermal path.
Vega governs the thermal path regardless of which door is on the rack. AGT's or yours — one system, one authority, one result.
Which means Vega deploys wherever cold water is needed. Existing facilities. New sites. Environments where no chiller plant is in scope. Continued on the next page →

borealis.cool/vega
Vega™
AGT-USA Inc.
Deployment Scenarios

Where Vega deploys — and why it fits there.

Vega addresses a specific thermal constraint at each site type — not by adapting to it, but by replacing the infrastructure path that creates the constraint in the first place. Six environments, one platform.

Colocation

Brownfield Retrofit

Existing colocation halls with tenant demand for liquid-cooled cabinets.
Thermal delivery is bounded by the current chilled-water plant; facility expansion becomes the critical path. Vega deploys inside the hall as a packaged unit — adding liquid-cooled capacity without central-plant expansion or tenant disruption.
Range500 kW – multi-MW per deployment
Enterprise

High-Density Pods

HPC, AI training, and inference clusters inside otherwise air-cooled enterprise data centers.
Enterprise IT owns the compute but rarely owns the facility. A single high-density pod cannot trigger a hall-wide thermal retrofit. Vega deploys alongside the pod, governed independently — adjacent racks, airflow patterns, and facility thermals remain untouched.
Range500 kW – 1 MW per pod
Hospital

On-Premises Data Centers

Hospital data halls supporting EMR, medical imaging, and clinical system infrastructure.
Existing chilled-water plants are committed to clinical loads — surgery, imaging, HVAC. Data center cooling cannot compete. Many hospitals have no data-center-specific thermal infrastructure at all. Vega brings its own thermal path, governed independently from clinical systems.
Typical100 kW – 500 kW
Lab

Research & Academic Compute

HPC, genomics, pharma, and scientific computing inside labs or academic research facilities.
Lab chilled-water systems are engineered around specialized process loads — sample prep, imaging, specialty gases — with flow rates and temperature stability commissioned to match. Adding GPU compute disrupts that equilibrium. Vega brings its own thermal path, isolating research compute from lab shared infrastructure.
Typical250 kW – 1 MW per cluster
Greenfield

AI Factory & GPU Buildout

Purpose-built AI and GPU facilities — often on former industrial sites, warehouses, or manufacturing buildings.
No existing data center infrastructure. Building a chilled-water plant from scratch becomes the project's critical path. Vega deploys as the thermal infrastructure itself — no central plant required.
ScaleStarting at 2 MW, phased
Edge

Space-Constrained & Modular

Regional edge sites, modular containerized builds, distributed compute at the network edge.
Edge sites fail far from the nearest technician. A traditional service-dispatch model does not scale across a distributed network. Vega's compact platform fits where facility chillers cannot, and service reaches the site through AGT's partner mechanical contractor network — not a factory thousands of miles away.
Scale100 kW – 1 MW per site
Cross-Cutting
Capability

No chilled-water infrastructure? Vega deploys ambient-first.

An adiabatic fluid cooler close-coupled to the delivery loop. Cold water at the load, regardless of the site. Warm-supply loads — including immersion — extend free-cooling hours further.

Configuration: Vega Direct
One platform, six environments — each resolved without a facility rebuild. Cold water produced at the load, inside a white-space footprint.
One platform, two configurations — continued on the next page →
Vega™ · AGT-USA Inc. · 04
borealis.cool/vega
Vega™
AGT-USA Inc.
Configuration 01

Vega Standard.

Architecture
Secondary Loop
Plate-HX Isolated
Isolated secondary-loop configuration

A plate heat exchanger isolates the IT fluid from the outdoor loop — filtered, stabilized, and governed. Optimized for mixed RDHx and direct-to-chip deployments inside a single thermal domain. Standard is pre-engineered for economizer integration, extending the isolated-loop architecture into an ambient-capable operating envelope while retaining plate-HX isolation.

Key Advantages
  • Platform-native scaling — architecture extends from 100 kW to 500 kW per unit; 500 kW flagship configuration shown.
  • Ambient-first upgrade path — pre-engineered for economizer integration, enabling full mechanical, ambient-first with trim, and ambient-only operation while preserving plate-HX isolation.
  • Protected thermal domain — outdoor-loop contamination and pressure events cannot cross the plate.
  • CleanCore™ — continuous fluid filtration and chemistry monitoring on the IT return, protecting cold-plate microchannels and RDHx coils.
  • Hybrid-capable — architected to support mixed RDHx and DTC loads in one governed loop.
  • Deep turndown — 5:1 (100 kW to 500 kW) through four staged compressors, hot-gas bypass, variable-speed pumping, and hydraulic buffering. No short-cycling at low load.
  • Unified supervisory control — one authority across compressors, pumps, valves, and heat rejection.
Full Compressor Mode
Dashed boundary = Vega internal scope
Process Supply
Compressor Refrigerant
Process Return
Condenser Heated Water
Fluid Cooler Return
Process & Condenser Water
Supervisory Control Signal
Inactive Flow Path
Configurable at Project Specification
Baseline architecture shown above. Configurations are specified at the project level — added where the deployment requires them.
Redundancy
  • Pump redundancy — parallel pump configuration with automatic failover.
  • Concurrent maintainability — architected so major components can be serviced without dropping thermal load.
  • Plate-HX redundancy — parallel plate heat exchangers with isolation valves.
Operating Envelope
  • Economizer integration — pre-engineered for ambient-first upgrade. See Key Advantages.
Best-Fit Contexts
  • Colocation brownfield — mixed RDHx and DTC tenant demand.
  • Enterprise pods — high-density clusters needing fluid isolation.
  • Hospital data centers — protected thermal domain, independent of clinical systems.
  • Retrofit sites with legacy cooling — Vega delivers a dedicated chiller plant for the IT zone; tie-in to existing facility systems where project scope requires.
Vega Standard is the isolated-loop configuration — one platform, protected thermal domain, mixed rack-side delivery.
For greenfield deployments and sites where no legacy plant is in scope, Vega deploys ambient-first. Continued on the next page →
Vega™ · AGT-USA Inc. · 05
borealis.cool/vega
Vega™
AGT-USA Inc.
Configuration 02

Vega Direct.

Architecture
Close-Coupled Direct Loop
Ambient-First
Ambient-first configuration

A close-coupled direct loop between the adiabatic fluid cooler and the rack-side delivery. No internal plate heat exchanger in the ambient path — zero approach-temperature penalty when ambient handles full duty. Refrigeration sits adjacent, engaging only when conditions require trim or mechanical assist.

Vega is the chiller plant — greenfield buildouts and sites where no legacy plant is in scope.
Three Governed Modes
  • Full Ambient — Adiabatic fluid cooler handles 100% of process cooling. Compressor idles. Maximum free-cooling hours, lowest PUE.
  • Hybrid Trim — Adiabatic handles base load. Refrigeration provides trim as ambient rises. The two paths blend continuously.
  • Full Mechanical — Refrigeration carries the cooling duty. Adiabatic fluid cooler provides condenser-water heat rejection only.
Best-Fit Contexts
  • Greenfield AI factory & GPU buildout — no central plant required; Vega is the thermal infrastructure.
  • Sites without legacy cooling plants — hospitals and labs deploying in spaces with no process-water commitment.
  • Regional edge & modular builds — compact deployment, partner-network service.
  • Warm-supply loads, including immersion — extends free-cooling hours further.
Full Ambient Mode Command · Full Adiabatic
Hybrid Trim Mode Command · Hybrid Trim
Full Mechanical Mode Command · Full Mechanical
Process Supply
Compressor Refrigerant
Process Return
Condenser Heated Water
Fluid Cooler Return
Process & Condenser Water
Supervisory Control Signal
Inactive Flow Path
Governed Mode Transitions
Vega selects the mode as ambient conditions change. One system, three governed modes, no manual intervention — compressor runtime minimized, delivery temperature held.

Scope & Operational Continuity

Ownership clarity after deployment.

What AGT delivers.
What the site provides.
How the platform stays supported.
01 Scope of Supply Typical Project Boundary
+ AGT Delivered Factory Scope
  • Vega packaged unit(s), factory-tested
  • Integrated supervisory controller, sensors, and internal control logic
  • Internal piping, pumps, valves, and refrigeration components
  • Internal plate heat exchanger in Standard configuration
  • UL 508A control panel
  • Factory wiring and packaged assembly
  • Remote air-cooled condenser or adiabatic fluid cooler — outdoor heat-rejection equipment, delivered as part of the chiller plant
  • AGT Rear-Door Heat Exchanger as matched pair, when included in project scope
  • Startup and commissioning support
  • Documentation and submittal package
· By Customer / Others Site Scope
  • Site preparation and housekeeping pads
  • Structural review and support as required
  • Electrical service to the unit
  • Network and BMS connection at site
  • Customer-preferred RDHx selection, specification, and procurement, if not using AGT matched pair
  • Roof penetrations, rigging, and weatherproofing
  • Outdoor heat-rejection equipment if not in AGT scope
  • Tie-in to existing facility chilled-water or condenser water systems, where the customer intends to leverage legacy capacity
  • Rack-side piping beyond AGT-defined interface if outside project scope
  • Building permits and local approvals
  • Ongoing facility water treatment where applicable
02 Service & Maintainability

Field service delivered through AGT's North American partner network of qualified mechanical and electrical service providers.

  • Front-access service — major components accessible without disassembly
  • Field-replaceable modules — pumps, sensors, and controls
  • Packaged architecture — reduces on-site diagnostic complexity
  • Documentation package supports in-house or partner service teams
03 Predictive Operations

Integrated supervisory control provides alarm handling, historical trending, remote diagnostics, component-level telemetry, and remote monitoring — supporting predictive maintenance and earlier fault detection.

  • Pump, temperature, and pressure telemetry
  • BACnet and Modbus integration to facility BMS
  • Redfish-ready rack telemetry where applicable
  • Remote access device installed on every unit — live monitoring where site policy permits; on-unit recording with retrievable logs in air-gapped environments
  • Fault telemetry supports proactive partner-network dispatch
04 Redundancy Architecture

Baseline architecture is single-pump, single plate-HX. Redundancy is specified at the project level.

  • Independent refrigerant circuits — baseline, four per unit
  • Pump redundancy — optional
  • Plate-HX redundancy — optional
  • Concurrent maintainability — optional
Commercial Clarity
A packaged platform with a defined project boundary, a field-service model that scales across North America, and governance telemetry that makes the system knowable in operation — not just on day one.
One platform. One governed thermal path. Thirty-five years of AGT-USA thermal engineering behind it. Continued on the next page →
Vega™ · AGT-USA Inc. · 08
borealis.cool/vega
Vega™
AGT-USA Inc.
Platform · Scaling · Deployment

One unit. One block. One facility.

Vega is defined at the unit — 100 to 500 kW per unit, packaged as a facility cooling platform. At the site, deployment remains modular: multiple Vega units compose into blocks sized to project demand, and the deployment extends to multi-MW scale through additional blocks. One architecture, scaled by composition rather than redesign.

Optional Turnkey White-Space Package

Everything inside the white space that touches the thermal upgrade.

Vega can anchor a complete white-space package. Working with qualified partners, AGT coordinates every component between the rack and the cooling plant — enclosures, rear-door heat exchangers, liquid distribution, power distribution, cable management, and governed cooling as one coordinated system — reducing multi-vendor integration scope.

AGT-supplied
Qualified partner
Either path available
GPU Rack Enclosures—Purpose-built or retrofit-compatible cabinets
Rear-Door Heat Exchangers—AGT-designed matched pair up to 70 kW, or customer-preferred RDHx supplied through qualified partners
Water-Cooled Terminal Devices—CRAH units, chilled beams, chilled walls, fan coils, and other water-cooled heat exchangers fed by Vega's facility water loop
Hot Aisle / Cold Aisle Containment—Engineered airflow separation, coordinated with Vega's thermal envelope
NexusLoop™—IT liquid loop distribution with leak detection
Power Distribution—PDUs sized and integrated to the deployment
Cable Management—Organized routing for power and data within the rack envelope
CleanCore™—Continuous fluid quality management and chemistry monitoring
Immersion Systems—Compatible single-phase or two-phase immersion, supplied at the thermal interface

Vega removes the thermal constraint and activates high-density deployment.

The Platform

One governed thermal path.

Facility-to-Chip™ Architecture
RDHx, DTC (single- or two-phase), enclosed thermal cabinets, and immersion all connect at the thermal interface.

Vega, from Borealis, is one packaged facility cooling platform that delivers cold water at the load — whether the site has chilled-water infrastructure or not. One governed thermal path, from site-flexible heat rejection to rack-side delivery. Scalable from 100 kW per unit through phased modular deployment — designed to scale with what compute demands next.

Thirty-Five Years of Precision Thermal Engineering
We've delivered precision thermal systems for thirty-five years. Thousands of deployed systems where temperature tolerance is measured in tenths of a degree — now applied to facility-class cooling.
Borealis, built on 35 years of AGT-USA Inc. thermal engineering experience. Vega is its current expression.
